text: Managing a team requires a balance of several key skills. First, clear communication is essential so that everyone understands their tasks and the team's goals. It is also important to delegate work effectively—assign tasks based on each member’s strengths and availability, which avoids burnout. Regular feedback helps team members improve and feel valued, while constructive criticism should be given respectfully. To keep motivation high, recognize achievements and celebrate small wins. Finally, when conflicts arise, address them quickly by listening to all sides and finding a fair solution. A leader who masters these areas can build a productive, positive, and united team.
